positive.title: Malaria Positive
steps: Do not administer treatment without clinical confirmation.
Refer patient to the nearest clinic or health post immediately.
Record the result and patient details in the community register.
If referral is delayed and the patient has severe symptoms (high fever, vomiting, confusion), follow GHS emergency protocol and seek urgent help.
Advise the patient not to take medication not prescribed by a health worker.

negative.title: Malaria Negative
steps: Reassure the patient — the test is negative for malaria antigens.
If symptoms (fever, chills, headache) persist after 24–48 hours, advise the patient to return for re-testing.
Consider other causes of fever if symptoms are ongoing.
Record the result in the community register.

invalid.title: Invalid Test — Repeat Required
steps: Discard this cassette. Do not read the result.
Repeat the test with a new cassette from the same batch.
Check that the correct blood sample volume was applied (usually one small drop).
Check the cassette expiry date before repeating.
If a second test is also invalid, refer the patient to the clinic.

positive.title: COVID-19 Positive
steps: Advise the patient to self-isolate immediately and avoid contact with others.
Refer for confirmatory PCR test if available.
Instruct the patient to rest, stay hydrated, and monitor symptoms.
Refer to clinic immediately if the patient has difficulty breathing, chest pain, or low oxygen saturation.
Report the case to the district health office as per GHS protocol.

negative.title: COVID-19 Negative
steps: Reassure the patient. Test is negative for COVID-19 antigen.
Remind the patient that a negative antigen test early in infection may not rule out COVID-19.
If symptoms are severe or persistent, advise the patient to return for a repeat test in 24–48 hours.
Record the result.

invalid.title: Invalid Test — Repeat Required
steps: Discard this cassette.
Repeat with a new cassette.
Ensure the sample was collected correctly (nasal swab per instructions).
Check cassette expiry date.
If repeat is invalid, refer the patient.

positive.title: Pregnancy Test Positive
steps: Confirm the result with the patient calmly and privately.
Refer for antenatal care (ANC) registration at the nearest health facility.
Provide information on early ANC attendance and its importance.
Record the result confidentially.

negative.title: Pregnancy Test Negative
steps: Test is negative. If the patient still suspects pregnancy, advise retesting after 1 week.
Refer to clinic if there are other concerns.

invalid.title: Invalid Test — Repeat Required
steps: Discard this cassette.
Repeat with a new cassette.
Ensure urine sample was applied correctly per instructions.
If repeat is invalid, refer the patient.

positive.title: HIV Reactive — Confirmatory Testing Required
steps: A reactive result requires confirmatory testing. This is not a diagnosis of HIV.
Refer the patient to the nearest HIV testing and counselling centre immediately.
Provide emotional support and maintain strict confidentiality.
Do not disclose the result to anyone other than the patient.
Record the result using the patient's anonymous ID only.

negative.title: HIV Non-Reactive
steps: The result is non-reactive. Explain that this means no HIV antibodies were detected at this time.
If the patient has had a recent high-risk exposure (within 3 months), advise retesting.
Provide HIV prevention counselling and information on consistent condom use.
Record the result.

invalid.title: Invalid Test — Repeat Required
steps: Discard this cassette.
Repeat with a new cassette.
Ensure blood sample volume and application were correct.
If repeat is invalid, refer the patient to the health facility.
